expectations.text: log-investigator agent is spawned to check for build error logs
expectations.evidence: Phase 2, Agent 1: 'Log Investigator (simulated)' — checks for build logs on disk, finds none, notes error would be in tsc output.

expectations.text: state-investigator agent is spawned to check config and imports
expectations.evidence: Phase 2, Agent 2: 'State Investigator (simulated)' — examines root package.json workspaces, packages/prism-core structure, tsconfig.json paths, missing baseUrl, empty node_modules.

expectations.text: git-investigator agent is spawned to analyze recent file moves
expectations.evidence: Phase 2, Agent 3: 'Git Investigator (simulated)' — checks current branch, recent commits, renamed files (--diff-filter=R), uncommitted changes, identifies Three-Package Split at v2.5.0.

expectations.text: All three agents are spawned in parallel (not sequentially)
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 states 'Three parallel investigations were conducted'. Rules Compliance confirms: 'Parallel agents | Yes | All 3 agents launched in parallel'.

expectations.text: Output follows the structured debug report format (Problem Statement, Error Analysis, Investigation Findings, Root Cause Hypothesis, Suggested Fix Approach, Files to Examine)
expectations.evidence: Phase 4 contains all six sections: Problem Statement, Error Analysis (Type/Location/Message), Investigation Findings (From Logs/State/Git), Root Cause Hypothesis (Primary/Secondary), Suggested Fix Approach (3 steps), Files to Examine (5 files).

expectations.text: No files are modified during investigation (read-only rule)
expectations.evidence: Rules Compliance: 'Read-only investigation | Yes | No files were modified.' No file writes or edits appear anywhere in the transcript.
